channel.title: Healthy Boundaries on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in Healthy Boundaries on MinWeb

item.title: Why You Should Stop Saying "We Need to Talk
item.description:
Have you ever received a text that simply said, “We need to talk,” and felt your stomach drop into your shoes?

Your heart starts racing. Your palms get sweaty. You spend the next three hours spiraling, convinced you’re getting fired, being broken up with, or that the world is ending.

If this happens to you, you aren’t alone. This phrase has earned a reputation as the “four-word disaster.” Honestly, it’s the most effective way to guarantee a conversation starts in a place of panic, defensiveness, and unnecessary emotional carnage.

item.title: Why You Should Stop Commenting on People’s Bodies
item.description:
The most important thing you can do for the people in your life is remarkably simple: mind your own business.

Stop commenting on bodies. This applies to the ones you think you are complimenting and the ones you find confusing. Even if you think you are being supportive, your words carry weight you cannot see and history you do not know.

_IMG_PLACEHOLDER

The unintended cost of your words

Saying “You look great, have you lost weight?” is not a harmless way to make someone feel good. A 2024 University of Bristol study found that weight teasing at age 13 predicts higher internalized weight stigma and poorer mental health twenty years later.
